conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a son, Alex. He's 17 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in college or anything like that. But he has started an apprenticeship recently.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. An apprenticeship can count as approved training in certain circumstances.

Is Alex's apprenticeship approved by the government (for example, is it a registered apprenticeship program)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's a proper apprenticeship here in England. He's doing training through a registered program.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to ask about your living situation:

Do you live in the UK, or are you otherwise subject to UK jurisdiction (for example, working for the UK government or EU institutions abroad)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand who is primarily responsible for Alex:

Are you the person mainly responsible for Alex? This typically means you live with him and are responsible for his day-to-day care and upbringing.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. He lives with me and I'm responsible for him day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex, or does Alex live with anyone else who might also be considered responsible for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and he just lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- You live in the UK
- You are the main person responsible for Alex and he lives with you
- Although Alex is 17, he is in a registered apprenticeship, which counts as approved training
- Child Benefit can be claimed for young people under 20 if they're in approved education or training
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You should be able to successfully apply for Child Benefit for your son. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply through the government's Child Benefit service.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and has started a registered apprenticeship in England. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for a child under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. An approved apprenticeship counts as approved training. The claimant lives in the UK, is the person mainly responsible for Alex, Alex lives with them, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, all eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. The claimant lives in the UK, is the main person responsible for Alex who lives with them, and Alex is in a registered apprenticeship which qualifies as approved training. No one else is claiming for Alex. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.
